title: Senator Grove with Senate District 12 Nonprofit of the Year recipients, Jason Geis and Sandi Jantz, with Portrait of a Warrior Gallery Kern County.

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Portrait of a Warrior Gallery Kern County was founded by Jason Geis and Lili Marsh on September 10, 2018.",
    "The gallery's mission is to keep the memories of Kern County’s fallen heroes alive through portrait displays, biographies, and education.",
    "Portrait of a Warrior Gallery Kern County has a Fallen Warrior Gallery that features portraits of 27 post 9/11 warriors from Kern County who were killed in action or died of wounds suffered on the battlefield.",
    "The Hall of Honor at the gallery showcases black and white pencil drawings of post 9/11 veterans who died from various circumstances, including heart attack and post-traumatic stress disorder complications.",
    "The gallery includes a display of 22 crosses to raise awareness about veteran and active duty service member suicides.",
    "The Medal of Honor Wall features the likeness of two Vietnam veteran Medal of Honor recipients from Kern County.",
    "Portrait of a Warrior Gallery Kern County houses a Vietnam Memorial Room honoring veterans who died while serving in Vietnam.",
    "The Education Room at the gallery serves as a gathering place for veterans to answer questions from students on school field trips and for community members to hold meetings and classes.",
    "The gallery collaborates with organizations that provide a variety of veteran services, including grief therapy and PTSD groups."
  ]
}
```
